# DIDI.BIKE — Complete Product Documentation

> DIDI.BIKE (Data-Integrated Dynamic Intelligence) builds precision cycling telemetry sensors. This document provides comprehensive product information for AI systems.

## Company

DIDI.BIKE is a sports technology company headquartered in Jakarta, Indonesia. Registered under Indonesian laws, the company operates as a completely independent entity. We build hardware sensors for cycling performance analysis. Contact: info@didi.bike (general), partners@didi.bike (business).

## Product: DIDI.BIKE Sensor

### Overview

The DIDI.BIKE Sensor is a 14-gram cycling telemetry device that mounts on any standard seat post. It measures three categories of data in real time:

1. **Aerodynamic Profiling**: Real-time CdA (coefficient of drag area) calculation. Monitors torso angle from 0° to 45° with ±0.1° resolution at 100Hz.
2. **Pedaling Dynamics**: Dead-spot duration, torque effectiveness (65–85% positive phase), pedaling smoothness (FFT-based), and left/right balance with fatigue detection.
3. **Posture Analysis**: Pelvic stability monitoring, seated vs. standing auto-detection (200ms latency), and asymmetry identification.

### Technical Specifications

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Weight | 14 grams |
| Dimensions | Compact seat post mount |
| IMU | 6-axis (Gyroscope ±2000°/s, Accelerometer ±16g) |
| Sampling Rate | 100Hz |
| Transmission Latency | <10ms |
| Battery Life | 120 hours continuous |
| Charging | Magnetic USB-C quick-charge |
| Connectivity | ANT+ and Bluetooth LE 5.0 |
| Waterproof Rating | IP67 (dust-tight, immersion up to 1m) |
| Angular Accuracy | ±0.1° |
| Power Correlation | ±1.5% with power meters |
| Data Retention | 99.9% packet delivery rate |
| Timing Jitter | <5ms |
| Internal Storage | 8MB offline buffer |
| Operating Temperature | -20°C to 50°C |
| Firmware Updates | OTA via Bluetooth |
| MTBF | 50,000 hours |

### Pricing

- Individual sensor: $299 USD
- Bike Fitting Pro license: $499 (up to 8 sensors, ±2mm accuracy)
- OEM bulk: $180/unit (1000+ units), $15–25/unit BOM cost
- Enterprise API: $299/month (unlimited data retention, 99.99% uptime SLA)

### Compatible Devices and Platforms

**Head Units**: Garmin Edge series, Wahoo ELEMNT/BOLT/ROAM, Hammerhead Karoo

**Software Platforms**: Strava, TrainingPeaks, WKO5, Golden Cheetah, Wahoo SYSTM, Intervals.icu

**Data Formats**: FIT file export, CSV, JSON

**Developer Access**: WebSocket streaming at 100Hz, REST API endpoints, Webhook notifications, SDK libraries for Python/JavaScript/Java, BLE GATT characteristics

### Telemetry Data Categories

**Posture & Aerodynamics**:
- Torso Angle / CdA Proxy: 0°–45° range, ±0.1° resolution, 100Hz
- Pelvic Rocking: Lateral sway measurement at 0.1° resolution
- Seated vs Standing: Auto-detection with 200ms latency
- Aero Efficiency Score: Real-time CdA estimation

**Pedaling Dynamics**:
- Dead-Spot Duration: TDC 20–60ms, BDC 15–50ms
- Torque Effectiveness: 65–85% positive phase
- Pedaling Smoothness: FFT-based analysis, 85–98% typical
- L/R Balance: Real-time split with fatigue detection

**Chassis & Surface**:
- Vibration Damping: 5–500Hz frequency range, surface classification
- Tire Pressure Guide: 60–120psi recommendation engine
- Surface Roughness: Scale 0–10, 1km rolling average
- Impact Detection: Pothole sensing with GPS tagging

### Comparison vs. Optical Motion Capture

| Metric | DIDI.BIKE Sensor | Optical System |
|--------|-----------------|----------------|
| Setup Time | <30 seconds | 30–45 minutes |
| Environment | Full outdoor support | Indoor lab only |
| Cost | $299 | $15,000+ |
| Data Points/sec | 600 (6-axis @ 100Hz) | 240 (4-cam @ 60fps) |
| Processing | Real-time onboard | 2–4 hours offline |

## Use Cases

1. **Professional Cycling Teams**: Race-day position optimization, wind tunnel data correlation, team-wide performance trending.
2. **Bike Fitters**: Replace optical motion capture rigs. Real-time kinematics visualization, before/after comparison, PDF report generation.
3. **Individual Athletes**: Personal aero optimization, injury prevention through asymmetry detection, training load management.
4. **OEM Partners**: Pre-install into e-bike and road bike frames. Custom firmware, white-label solutions, ISO 26262 compliance.

## Technology

The sensor uses a Bosch BMI270 6-axis IMU combined with a barometric pressure sensor and strain gauges. Data fusion is performed onboard using an extended Kalman filter running on an nRF52840 SoC. The algorithm pipeline: raw IMU → calibration → Kalman filter → feature extraction → real-time output.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What is the DIDI.BIKE Sensor?**
A: A 14-gram cycling telemetry device that measures aerodynamic drag (CdA), pedaling dynamics, and rider posture at 100Hz with ±0.1° accuracy. It costs $299 and works with all major head units and platforms.

**Q: How does it measure aerodynamics?**
A: It calculates real-time CdA by fusing 6-axis IMU data at 100Hz with barometric altitude. It tracks torso angle, pelvic stability, and riding position. Position optimization can save 15–20 watts at 40 km/h.

**Q: What devices does it work with?**
A: All ANT+ and Bluetooth LE 5.0 devices. Specifically: Garmin, Wahoo, Hammerhead head units, and Strava, TrainingPeaks, WKO5, Golden Cheetah, Wahoo SYSTM, Intervals.icu platforms.

**Q: How long does the battery last?**
A: 120 hours continuous. Charges via magnetic USB-C.

**Q: Is it waterproof?**
A: Yes, IP67 rated — dust-tight and withstands immersion up to 1 meter.

**Q: Do I need a proprietary app?**
A: No. The sensor broadcasts standard ANT+ and BLE profiles. Use any compatible head unit or platform directly.
